claim: The universal sensorium hooks (SessionStart/PostCompact gen_spec, UserPromptSubmit emit_cipher+claude_md_diff_watch+attention_hook, PreToolUse graph-guard, Stop context_producer) shall live in a committed project settings.json generated by a setup tool with commands portable via $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR, never only in the personal git-ignored settings.local.json.
why: Not yet ported in the Go port. The historical generator was tools/setup_hooks.py, which produced the committed project settings.json (the portable sensorium) so that a fresh clone got the pulse and the graph-guard rather than starting blind. setup-hooks is Declared (not ported) in methodology.Tools, and the adapter tools it wired (attention-hook, claude-md-diff-watch, context-producer, emit-cipher) are all Declared/not-ported as well. The original WHY stands: hooks that live only in the personal git-ignored settings.local.json leave a fresh clone with no pulse and no graph-guard. A committed .claude/settings.json exists in the repo, but it is not generated by a ported setup tool and does not carry the full Python sensorium enumeration; the portable-sensorium generator and its hook adapters are unported substrate. PROSE (not ENFORCED): no enforcer exists in the Go port.
